hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions in the ECL2/TM5 pocket while avoiding off-target SSTR3/5 binding. This will increase the selectivity margin by improving SSTR2 complementarity and reducing off-target affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is not sufficiently disrupting off-target interactions. We hypothesize that introducing specific mutations at non-pharmacophore positions will enhance SSTR2 selectivity without compromising binding affinity.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, we need to disrupt conserved pocket contacts in SSTR3/SSTR5 at non-pharmacophore positions. Focusing on these positions allows us to optimize for SSTR2 specificity.
These mutations are designed to break conserved interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 while preserving the pharmacophore (FWKT at positions 7-10) and disulfide bridge (Cys3/Cys14).
